items.title: Ranking Every Commitment from Minnesota's Busy Official Visit Weekend - Sports Illustrated
items.summary: Add us as a preferred source. The Gophers are coming off their first official visit weekend of the 2027 cycle. Miles became Minnesota's first flip of the class when he changed his decision from Michigan to the Gophers. I am very bullish on his long-term potential at the college level. He's listed as an athlete (ATH) on the recruiting services, but Minnesota pursued him as a safety. He had 51 catches for 863 yards and seven touchdowns as a junior, and I think his recruiting ranking could quickly rise with a big senior season. If I had to pick one offensive lineman to develop into a starter out of the five currently committed to Minnesota's 2027 class, I would land on Mota. He has production and elite track speed, which gives him the potential to contribute at the next level, but I have worries about his size. He has the size and athleticism to be a Big Ten player, but he's still raw at the position.

items.title: The next move for a frustrating Manchester City winger is becoming clearer - Yahoo Sports
items.summary: It has now been reported that Tottenham is making a strong move to sign Savinho. It seems that it is now a matter of time before Savinho leaves Manchester City. Fabrizio Romano has reported that Tottenham is making progress in its bid to sign Savinho from Manchester City this summer. It is also reported that City could sell the Brazilian international if a good proposal arrives from Tottenham. ## Savinho could be the first domino to fall in terms of who leaves Manchester City this summer. It does appear like it could be a busy summer for Manchester City in terms of players leaving the club. Savinho could be one of the first players to leave City this summer. As long as Spurs meet Manchester City’s asking price, it does seem like Savinho could be one of the first players to leave City in what looms as a busy summer at the FA Cup and Carabao Cup holders.
